what_it_is: A provisional runbook that proposes a coverage-first, pre-build methodology for scanning, digesting, and understanding a codebase before design and builder dispatch.
what_it_does: It lays out a phased workflow (boundary scan → full coverage → layered summaries → design proposal → option/verify panels → builder dispatch), assigns existing and proposed tools/roles to each phase, refines the approach with structured-output and deterministic-lifter requirements, and ends with decisions awaiting Tim's approval.
observations: Coverage is defined as an open-ended per-file digest of what each file IS, not a search for specific items.
The directory boundary is treated as the objective marker and denominator for coverage.
It inventories existing roles (repo_digest, interpret_file, reduce_synth, spec-compiler, option-panel, verify-jury, etc.) and existing flows/cascades.
It proposes a new boundary-scanner MCP tool and a coverage manifest that records per-region file counts, types, already-covered counts, and gaps.

Phase 0 is boundary/hygiene; Phase 1 is full per-file coverage with tiered model allocation; Phase 2 builds layered understanding; Phase 3 runs the spec-compiler; Phase 4 runs option and verify panels; Phase 5 dispatches builders.
A REUSE vs NEW section honestly separates already-built tooling from tooling that must still be built.

Refinements dated 2026-06-17 replace a flat digest plan with a layered pass: Pass 0 programmatic scan/ignore-list, Pass 1 deterministic lifters, Pass 2 schema-enforced model digest, Pass 3 embed and ledger.
Model-tier routing is grounded in internal SEM research: resident 4B swarm for extraction/census, cloud models for judgment/confirmation, embeddings + reranker for recall.
The file repeatedly notes it is PROVISIONAL and awaits Tim's OK on the compute run and on adopting the methodology as standard.
It includes a [[FORMS-ARE-PLACEHOLDER]] warning and says forms taxonomy should emerge from coverage, not be locked now.
standouts: The refined layered pass (programmatic scan → deterministic lifters → schema-enforced digest → chunked embed) is the central methodological hardening.
Tiered model routing explicitly assigns cheap resident swarm to extraction, cloud/judgment models to confirmation/synthesis, and embeddings/reranker to recall.
The coverage manifest is proposed as the objective denominator against which completeness is measured.
The document is framed as a decision memo to Tim with three explicit OKs requested.
conventions: Uses phase numbering (0–5) and a REUSE/NEW split.
References internal role names in bold and internal flow/cascade names in backticks.
Cites specific source locations (e.g., fabric/transport.py, cognition.py:1431) and internal wiki links (e.g., [[FORMS-ARE-PLACEHOLDER]]).
Marks unresolved work with TODO and provisional status.
Breaks the typical runbook convention by being meta-methodological and embedding a proposal/approval request rather than pure instructions.
concerns: It is explicitly provisional and cannot be executed until Tim approves the compute run and methodology.
Several key terms (repo_digest, ingest, run_items, FormRegistry.route, etc.) are named but not defined inside this file.
The vendored-tree anomaly and the true source boundary are unresolved TODOs.
Model-tier routing references SEM-1/2/3 research that is not included here, so the rationale is not self-contained.
The forms taxonomy is admitted to be a placeholder, leaving a structural dependency unresolved.
notes: A provisional, methodology-heavy runbook that proposes a phased, tiered-model coverage pipeline to digest the whole repo before design, awaiting Tim's approval and a compute run.
questions: What are the exact interfaces and output schemas of the listed roles (repo_digest, ingest, run_items, run_reduce, etc.)?
Which directories/files constitute the vendored anomaly and how will the ignore-list be generated?
What is the precise JSON schema used for the structured-output enforcement in Pass 2?
What do the SEM-1/2/3 findings specifically conclude about extraction vs judgment?
What criteria determine whether a region/file is routed to the local swarm, cloud models, or a Claude Code clone?
What format will the coverage manifest take and where will it be persisted?
What does Tim's OK specifically require for each of the three decisions?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated as a pre-build runbook; the file is largely a design proposal and methodology checklist awaiting approval and tooling, not yet a self-contained executable runbook.
apparent_themes: Coverage as an objective, measurable boundary marker
Reuse existing tooling before building net-new
Tiered model routing matched to extraction vs judgment
Deterministic structure extraction before model inference
intention_signals: It is trying to establish a standardized, repeatable pre-build ritual that provably covers the whole codebase, separates cheap extraction from careful judgment, and feeds complete understanding into design panels and builder dispatch, replacing ad-hoc search with a coverage ledger.
novelty: It explicitly defines a coverage-first design pipeline with a coverage-manifest denominator, separates extraction (resident 4B swarm) from judgment (cloud), mandates deterministic lifters before model digest, and embeds a personal decision checklist directed at Tim.
contribution: The file contributes a structured pre-build coverage methodology, a phased workflow, a tiered model-routing rationale, a refined layered pass, and an explicit decision checklist; it also records the provisional status and unresolved boundary/ignore-list issues that must be resolved before execution.
summary_for_embedding: Coverage-First Pre-Build Runbook is a provisional methodology document that lays out a phased, tiered-model pipeline for boundary-scanning, digesting, summarizing, and designing from complete codebase coverage before dispatching builders.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to propose and standardize a coverage-first pre-build ritual, grounded in existing tools and Tim's refinements, and to obtain approval for a compute run and methodology adoption.
